critical.mandate: Story files must include complete YAML frontmatter with ALL required fields: key, title, epic, status, priority, size, points, risk, sprint_id, depends_on, blocks, traces_to, date, author, priority_flag. The priority_flag field accepts only null (default) or "next-sprint" as valid values.
All acceptance criteria MUST use Given/When/Then format: "Given {context}, when {action}, then {expected result}"
Check web access availability if story references external APIs

step.title: Select Story
step.action: Read {planning_artifacts}/epics-and-stories.md — parse all stories with their keys, titles, priorities, sizes, risk levels, and dependencies.
Scan {implementation_artifacts}/ for existing story files matching *-*.md patterns. Record which story keys already have individual files.
Build a list of stories that do NOT yet have individual story files.
Sort the list by: Priority (P0 first, then P1, then P2), then dependency topology (stories with no dependencies first).

check.if: user provided a story key (e.g., /gaia-create-story E1-S2)

step.check: Use the provided story key directly — skip the selection list. Verify the key exists in epics-and-stories.md. If a story file already exists for this key: 1. Read its YAML frontmatter status field 2. If status is 'backlog': warn "Story file exists with status backlog. Proceeding will regenerate it." Allow continue. 3. If status is anything else (validating, ready-for-dev, in-progress, invalid, review, done): HALT: "Story {key} is in '{status}' status. Overwriting would destroy work in progress. - To edit the story: use /gaia-fix-story {key} - To reset to backlog and recreate: manually set frontmatter status to 'backlog' first" Do NOT proceed — this is a hard gate. Even in YOLO mode, do NOT bypass this check.

Display the prioritized list of stories without files as a table: | # | Key | Title | Priority | Size | Risk | Depends On | Mark P0 stories with a ⚠ indicator. Which story do you want to create? Select from the list above (provide the story key).

step.title: Load Context
step.action: Read story summary from epics-and-stories.md
Read architecture.md for technical context

step.title: Web Access Guard
step.check: Check if web tools available for API documentation lookup

step.title: Elaborate Story
step.action: Present a brief summary of what was loaded from epics and architecture context for this story.
In YOLO mode: auto-select [a] — let PM (Derek) and Architect (Theo) answer based on project artifacts. Skip the elaboration method prompt below.
step.ask: How would you like to elaborate this story? [u] I'll answer the questions myself [a] Let PM (Derek) and Architect (Theo) answer based on project artifacts

check.ask: Are there specific edge cases or error scenarios to call out in the acceptance criteria?
Any implementation preferences or constraints not in the architecture? (libraries, patterns, performance targets)
Should any acceptance criteria be split out or deprioritized?
Any additional context, stakeholder notes, or dependencies to include?

check.action:
Spawn two subagents in parallel using the Agent tool (both calls in a single message):
PM subagent (Derek): "You are Derek, the Product Manager. Read the following files: - {planning_artifacts}/epics-and-stories.md - {planning_artifacts}/prd.md (if exists) - {planning_artifacts}/ux-design.md (if exists) For story {story_key} ({story_title}), answer these questions: 1. What specific edge cases or error scenarios should be called out in the acceptance criteria? 2. Should any acceptance criteria be split out or deprioritized? Why? 3. Any additional stakeholder notes, context, or dependencies to include? Return your answers in a structured format."
Architect subagent (Theo): "You are Theo, the System Architect. Read the following files: - {planning_artifacts}/architecture.md - {test_artifacts}/test-plan.md (if exists) - {planning_artifacts}/epics-and-stories.md For story {story_key} ({story_title}), answer these questions: 1. Any implementation preferences or constraints not captured in the architecture? (libraries, patterns, performance targets) 2. Any technical dependencies or additional context to include? Return your answers in a structured format."
When both subagents return: merge responses into a consolidated summary. Present to user:
In YOLO mode: auto-continue with the subagent responses — skip the [c]/[e]/[a] prompt below.

step.action: Incorporate responses (user's or agents') into the story elaboration
Expand acceptance criteria in Given/When/Then format: "Given {context}, when {action}, then {expected result}"
Add technical notes from architecture context
Define subtasks for implementation
step.check: Skip edge case analysis sub-step — story is small enough that edge case analysis would waste token budget. Log to Dev Notes: "Edge case analysis: skipped (size=S)". Set edge_case_results = [].
Edge case analysis sub-step is mandatory for this story size. Proceed to Step 4b (Edge Case Analysis Sub-Step) below. Log to Dev Notes: "Edge case analysis: completed (size={size})"

step.title: Edge Case Analysis Sub-Step (M/L/XL only)

critical.mandate: This sub-step implements FR-227: /gaia-create-story MUST invoke the edge-cases skill as a mandatory sub-step for M+ stories after acceptance criteria are drafted. The sub-step runs in-context — it does NOT use invoke-workflow or invoke-task. It is a JIT skill load only.
Token budget: the entire sub-step (input context + skill output) MUST stay within 8K tokens (NFR-042). If the budget is exceeded, truncate results with a warning logged to Dev Notes.

Sub-step failure is NON-BLOCKING: if the edge case skill fails, times out, or returns malformed output, log a warning and continue story creation with edge_case_results = []. Story file write MUST NOT be blocked by edge case sub-step failure.

step.check: Skip this entire step — the size gate in Step 4 already excluded S-sized stories. Proceed to Step 5.
step.action: JIT-load the edge case analysis skill from {project-root}/_gaia/dev/skills/edge-cases.md. Do NOT use invoke-workflow or invoke-task — this is a direct skill load in the current create-story context. The skill file must exist at that path; if missing, log warning "Edge case skill not loaded — continuing without edge cases", set edge_case_results = [], and proceed to Step 5 (non-blocking).

Build the input context for the edge-cases skill per its input-contract section: story_key, story_title, story_description, acceptance_criteria (the list drafted in Step 4), size, and an optional architecture_excerpt (only if directly relevant). Keep the input context under 5K tokens to stay within the NFR-042 8K total budget.

Invoke the edge case analysis inline, following the edge-cases.md skill heuristics. Generate a structured list of edge cases using the skill's output-schema. Each edge case MUST include all five required fields: id, scenario, input, expected, category. The id uses EC-{N} sequential numbering (EC-1, EC-2, ...). The category field uses one of the enum values defined in edge-cases.md (boundary, error, timing, concurrency, integration, security, data, environment).

Store the skill's structured output in a named runtime variable `edge_case_results` — a list of objects each with {id, scenario, input, expected, category}. This variable MUST be captured and stored BEFORE the Step 6 template-output writes the story file to disk, satisfying the completion gate (AC5). Example: edge_case_results = [{id: "EC-1", scenario: "...", input: "...", expected: "...", category: "boundary"}, ...].

Validate the structured output: confirm every entry has all five required fields (id, scenario, input, expected, category). If any entry is missing a required field, treat the output as malformed per the skill's failure-handling section — log warning "Edge case output schema invalid — continuing without edge cases", set edge_case_results = [], and proceed (non-blocking).

TOKEN BUDGET CHECK (NFR-042): After the skill returns, estimate the total tokens consumed by (input context + skill output). If the total exceeds the 8K NFR-042 token budget, truncate edge_case_results — drop lower-priority entries (keep boundary + error + security first) and log warning to Dev Notes: "Edge case output truncated at 8K tokens — N results dropped". If token usage reaches 80% of budget (6.4K) without exceeding, log a note to Dev Notes: "Edge case sub-step token usage: {used}K / 8K".

TIMEOUT / FAILURE GUARD (AC6): Wrap the entire edge case sub-step invocation in an error handler. If the skill fails for ANY reason — skill file not found, timeout (> 30s wall clock), exception during analysis, malformed output, or token budget blowout — log a warning "Edge case analysis failed: {reason} — continuing without edge cases", set edge_case_results = [], and proceed to Step 5. This sub-step is explicitly non-blocking: edge case failure MUST NOT halt story creation. This is the only place in create-story where a sub-step failure is allowed to warn-and-continue instead of HALT.

COMPLETION GATE (AC5): Confirm that edge_case_results has been captured (either as a populated list or as an empty list after failure). Only after this confirmation may the workflow proceed to Step 5 and eventually Step 6's template-output. If edge_case_results is unset (not merely empty), treat as a malformed invocation and reset to []. Log to Dev Notes: "Edge case sub-step complete: {count} results captured".

Persist edge_case_results into the story for traceability: the Step 6 template-output appends a summary of the captured edge cases into the story's Test Scenarios section (or Dev Notes if Test Scenarios is not applicable). Each edge case is rendered as a row with its id, scenario, input, expected, and category — preserving the structured shape.

step.title: Append Edge Case Results to Acceptance Criteria (FR-229)

critical.mandate: This sub-step implements FR-229: edge case results captured by Step 4b MUST be appended to the story's Acceptance Criteria section as AC-EC{N} items BEFORE Step 6 writes the story file to disk. This ensures edge cases are formally tracked and verifiable at review time.

Primary ACs are IMMUTABLE at this point — never modify, never reorder, never insert between existing primary ACs. AC-EC items are append-only, added AFTER the last primary AC in the Acceptance Criteria section. This is the AC4 safety guarantee.

AC-EC numbering restarts from 1 per story. Each story's AC-EC counter is independent of other stories and independent of the primary AC numbers — there is no collision because the AC-EC prefix is distinct.

step.check: Skip this entire step — Step 4's size gate already excluded S-sized stories from edge case analysis. Proceed to Step 5.
step.action: SNAPSHOT PRIMARY ACs (AC4): Before processing edge_case_results, capture the list of primary ACs drafted in Step 4 into a frozen variable primary_acs_snapshot. This snapshot is read-only for the remainder of the workflow. After the AC-EC append completes, verify that the first len(primary_acs_snapshot) entries in the Acceptance Criteria list still match the snapshot exactly (same order, same text). If any primary AC text or order has changed, HALT with "AC4 violation: primary AC list was modified during edge case append." This safety check ensures primary ACs are never touched.

ZERO-RESULT FAST PATH (AC5): If edge_case_results is empty (length == 0 or the list is []) — either because the edge case sub-step returned no results, failed non-blocking, or was skipped — do NOT add any AC-EC items to the Acceptance Criteria section. Append the note "No edge cases identified." to the story's Dev Notes section and proceed to Step 5. The Acceptance Criteria section remains exactly as drafted in Step 4.

FORMAT EACH EDGE CASE AS AC-EC (AC1, AC2, AC3): For each entry in edge_case_results, format a new acceptance criterion using the canonical AC-EC rendering template: - [ ] **AC-EC{N}:** {scenario} — Expected: {expected} [category: {category}] where: - {N} is a sequential counter starting at 1 for this story (AC-EC1, AC-EC2, AC-EC3, ...). The counter restarts from 1 for every story — there is no global AC-EC numbering and no collision with primary AC numbers because the "AC-EC" prefix is distinct from the primary "AC" prefix. - {scenario} is the scenario field from the edge case result (a short natural-language description of the edge case). - {expected} is the expected field from the edge case result (the observable outcome that verifies the edge case is handled). - {category} is the category field from the edge case result (one of the enum values: boundary, error, timing, concurrency, integration, security, data, environment). The rendered line must include all three fields (scenario, expected, category) in exactly this order. The bracketed `[category: {category}]` tag is part of the canonical format so reviewers can quickly triage edge-case ACs by category.

APPEND AFTER LAST PRIMARY AC (AC4): Insert the formatted AC-EC lines immediately AFTER the last primary AC in the Acceptance Criteria section — never before, never between primary ACs, never in a separate section. The final Acceptance Criteria list order is: [all primary ACs in their original order, then AC-EC1, AC-EC2, ..., AC-ECn]. Do NOT renumber or rewrite any primary AC. Do NOT alter the spacing, formatting, or text of any primary AC. The append is strictly additive.

NUMBERING CONSISTENCY (Task 4): Confirm the AC-EC counter started from 1 for this story and that no AC-EC number collides with a primary AC number (e.g., if the story has primary ACs AC1..AC5, the edge case ACs are AC-EC1..AC-ECn, NOT AC6..). The "AC-EC" prefix is the canonical disambiguator — never drop it, never merge AC-EC numbering into the primary AC sequence.

DEV NOTES LOG: Append to the story's Dev Notes section: "Edge case AC append: {count} AC-EC items added (FR-229)". If count is 0, the zero-result fast path above has already logged "No edge cases identified." — do not double-log.

POST-APPEND PRIMARY AC VERIFICATION (AC4): Re-read the Acceptance Criteria section after the append and compare the first len(primary_acs_snapshot) items against primary_acs_snapshot. They MUST match exactly. If not, HALT with "AC4 violation: primary ACs were modified during AC-EC append." This is the final safety gate before the workflow proceeds to Step 5.

step.title: Append Edge Case Results to Test Plan (FR-230)

critical.mandate: This sub-step implements FR-230: edge case results captured by Step 4b MUST be appended to {test_artifacts}/test-plan.md as new rows BEFORE Step 6 writes the story file to disk. This ensures edge case scenarios are captured in the test artifact, not only in the story file.
Sub-step failure is NON-BLOCKING (AC4): if test-plan.md does not exist, log a note in the story's Dev Notes and continue without error. Story creation MUST NOT be blocked by a missing test-plan.md.
Idempotency is REQUIRED (AC5): re-running create-story for the same story_key MUST NOT duplicate edge case rows in the test plan. Dedup key = (story_key, scenario).

step.check: Skip this entire step — Step 4's size gate already excluded S-sized stories from edge case analysis. Proceed to Step 5.
step.action: ZERO-RESULT FAST PATH: If edge_case_results is empty (length == 0) — either because the edge case sub-step returned no results, failed non-blocking, or was skipped — do NOT open or modify test-plan.md. Proceed to Step 5. No-op, no dev note (the AC-EC append step 4c already logged "No edge cases identified.").

FILE EXISTENCE CHECK (AC4): Check whether {test_artifacts}/test-plan.md exists. If the file does not exist (file absent, path not found, or the directory is missing), log a note in the story's Dev Notes section — exactly "Edge case test plan append: skipped — test-plan.md not found at {test_artifacts}/test-plan.md (non-blocking)." Do NOT raise an error, do NOT create test-plan.md, do NOT halt. Proceed to Step 5. This is the non-blocking fallback path.
READ TEST PLAN: Read {test_artifacts}/test-plan.md into memory. The content is the authoritative target for both the existing-section locate and the highest-TC-ID scan below.

LOCATE STORY SECTION (AC1): Search the test-plan.md content for a heading or row that references this story_key. Locate the story's section via any of: (a) a markdown heading containing the story_key (e.g., "### E19-S11 — ..." or "## {story_key}"); (b) an existing table row in the test-plan.md whose trailing cell contains the story_key. If a section is found, remember the insertion point (immediately after the last existing row of that section). If no section is found, the insertion point is the end of the file — a new section will be created.

TC ID NUMBERING (AC3): Scan the entire test-plan.md content for the highest existing test case ID. Match the canonical TC ID patterns used by the test plan (e.g., TC-{N}, TGA-{N}, ECI-{N}, and any other prefix already present in the file). For the purposes of this append, find the highest numeric suffix among all IDs sharing the same prefix the story's Dev Notes declares (e.g., ECI-13 for E19-S11 → next is ECI-14). If no prefix is declared in Dev Notes, default to TC-{N} and auto-increment from the highest existing TC-{N}. If no TC IDs exist at all, start at TC-1. The next TC ID assigned to the first new edge case row is (highest + 1), and subsequent edge cases increment sequentially.

IDEMPOTENCY / DEDUP CHECK (AC5): Before appending any row, scan the test-plan.md content for existing rows that match this (story_key, scenario) pair. A row is considered a duplicate if its trailing cell contains the story_key AND its scenario cell contains the exact scenario text from the current edge_case_results entry. For each edge case result: if a duplicate row exists, SKIP that entry (do not append); otherwise, mark it for append. Track the count of skipped duplicates and the count of new rows to append. The dedup key is (story_key, scenario) — this makes re-runs of create-story for the same story_key idempotent.

FORMAT ROWS (AC2): For each non-duplicate edge case result, format a new test-plan row using the canonical row format: | {tc_id} | {scenario} | edge-case | {severity} | {story_key} | where: - {tc_id} is the auto-incremented test case ID from the numbering step above (e.g., ECI-14, ECI-15). - {scenario} is the scenario field from the edge case result (verbatim from edge_case_results). - edge-case is the literal tag string — never replaced, never translated, never lower/upper-cased differently. - {severity} is derived from the edge case category: boundary/data → medium; error/integration → high; security → high; timing/concurrency → high; environment → low. If category is missing or unmapped, default to medium. - {story_key} is the current story's key (e.g., E19-S11) — this is the dedup key used in AC5. The row columns MUST appear in exactly this order: tc_id | scenario | edge-case | severity | story_key. Never reorder.

WRITE TEST PLAN: Write the updated test-plan.md content back to {test_artifacts}/test-plan.md. Preserve the original file's trailing newline convention and do not touch any content outside the insertion point. If the write fails for any reason (permission denied, disk full, etc.), log a warning to Dev Notes "Edge case test plan append: write failed — {error}. Scenarios preserved in story file only." and proceed non-blockingly.

DEV NOTES LOG: Append to the story's Dev Notes section exactly: "Edge case test plan append: {new_count} rows added, {dup_count} duplicates skipped (FR-230)". If both counts are zero (everything was a duplicate on a re-run), the line is still logged so the idempotency path is observable.

COMPLETION GATE: Confirm the test plan append step has run to completion (either by appending rows, skipping on missing file, skipping on zero results, or logging a write failure). Only after this confirmation may the workflow proceed to Step 5.

step.title: Definition of Done
step.action: Append DoD checklist: all AC met, tests pass, code reviewed, docs updated

step.title: Generate Output
step.action: Read the story template from the engine-resolved template path. The engine resolves this in Step 1 (Load and Resolve Config): if {project-root}/custom/templates/story-template.md exists and is non-empty, the custom template is used; otherwise it falls back to {project-root}/_gaia/lifecycle/templates/story-template.md. Use whichever path the engine resolved.
Read sizing_map from {project-root}/_gaia/_config/global.yaml to resolve T-shirt size to story points (S→2, M→5, L→8, XL→13).

Detect invocation context to determine origin fields: If invoked from problem-solving routing (E16-S3): set origin="problem-solving" and origin_ref to the path of the Problem Brief or problem-solving checkpoint artifact (e.g., docs/creative-artifacts/problem-solving-YYYY-MM-DD.md). If invoked from triage routing: set origin="triage" and origin_ref to the triage artifact path. If invoked from add-feature routing: set origin="add-feature" and origin_ref to the source artifact path. If invoked from sprint-planning: set origin="sprint-planning" and origin_ref to the sprint plan artifact path. If invoked with explicit origin parameters from caller: use the provided origin and origin_ref values. If invoked normally (no routing context): set origin=null and origin_ref=null (planned work default).

Populate ALL YAML frontmatter fields from epics-and-stories.md data: - key: story key from epics (e.g., E1-S1) - title: story title - epic: epic key and name (e.g., "E1 — Framework Core") - status: backlog (always — sprint assignment happens later in /gaia-sprint-plan) - priority: P0/P1/P2 from epics - size: S/M/L/XL from epics - points: resolved from sizing_map (e.g., L → 8) - risk: high/medium/low from epics or test-plan risk levels - sprint_id: null (assigned by /gaia-sprint-plan) - depends_on: list of story keys this story depends on (from epics) - blocks: list of story keys this story blocks (from epics) - traces_to: requirement IDs this story traces to (from epics) - date: current date - author: agent name (e.g., "Nate (Scrum Master)") - priority_flag: null (default — set to "next-sprint" by add-feature for high-urgency stories) - origin: workflow origin (null for planned work, "problem-solving" from problem-solving routing, "triage" from triage, "add-feature" from add-feature routing, "sprint-planning" from sprint planning, "manual" for explicit manual creation) - origin_ref: path to source artifact that triggered story creation (null when origin is null)
step.template-output: Generate the story file following the story-template.md structure. The filename must use the story key and slugified title (e.g., E1-S1-user-login.md). Include complete YAML frontmatter with ALL 15 fields populated. Fill all template sections: User Story, Acceptance Criteria, Tasks/Subtasks (linked to AC numbers), Dev Notes, Technical Notes, Dependencies, Test Scenarios, Project Structure Notes, References, Dev Agent Record, and Estimate. IMPORTANT: The body "**Status:**" line MUST match the frontmatter status field exactly. Both must say the same status value.
step.action: Add the new story to {implementation_artifacts}/story-index.yaml: 1. Read story-index.yaml (create with header if doesn't exist) 2. Add entry for {story_key}: title, epic (extract key from epic field), status (backlog), sprint_id (null), priority, risk, author, file ({story_key}-{story_title_slug}.md) 3. Update last_updated timestamp 4. Non-blocking — if write fails, log warning and continue

step.title: Val Full Validation
step.action:
Invoke Val as a subagent to perform full validation on the saved story file. Val validates:
(a) Completeness — all 15 YAML frontmatter fields present and valid, all template sections present (b) Clarity — all ACs in Given/When/Then format, subtasks linked to AC numbers, DoD items measurable (c) Semantic quality — duplicate detection across epic, ADR cross-references against architecture.md (d) Dependencies — all declared dependencies exist and resolve (e) Factual verification — extract verifiable claims (file paths, component references, API endpoints, versions), verify against filesystem and ground truth, classify as CRITICAL/WARNING/INFO
Val must load: the saved story file, {planning_artifacts}/architecture.md, {planning_artifacts}/epics-and-stories.md, and {memory_path}/validator-sidecar/ground-truth.md (if exists). Error guard: if Val fails or crashes, log warning, keep status as backlog, and skip to Step 9.

check.action: Verify: re-read the story file and confirm that BOTH the frontmatter status field AND the body "**Status:**" line say "ready-for-dev". If the body still shows a different status, update it directly. This prevents frontmatter/body drift.
Report "Validation passed — story is ready for dev." Proceed to Step 9.
Present findings table to user (severity, section, claim, finding, evidence).

SM (Nate) addresses each finding: - For each CRITICAL or WARNING finding: fix the story content directly - For each INFO finding: fix or acknowledge with a note Present the corrected story sections to user for confirmation.
After SM applies all fixes, write the updated story file. Increment validation_attempt.

Re-invoke Val validation on the updated story (same invoke-workflow as Step 7). - If Val returns zero findings: invoke status-sync with new_status='ready-for-dev'. Exit loop. Proceed to Step 9. - If Val returns new findings AND validation_attempt < 3: SM fixes again, Val re-validates. - If Val returns findings AND validation_attempt = 3 (hard limit reached): keep status as 'validating', report "Validation limit reached — {N} findings remain. Run /gaia-validate-story {story_key} to continue." Proceed to Step 9.

step.title: Save to Val Memory
step.action:
Auto-save all validation results to Val's memory sidecar (no user prompt required):
1. Append to {memory_path}/validator-sidecar/decision-log.md using standardized format: ### [YYYY-MM-DD] Story Validation: {story_key}
- **Agent:** validator - **Workflow:** create-story (post-save validation) - **Sprint:** {sprint_id or "N/A"} - **Status:** recorded - **Related:** {story_key}
Validated story {story_key} ({story_title}). Result: {PASS or FAIL after N attempts}. Findings: {total_count} total, {fixed_count} fixed, {remaining_count} remaining. Final status: {ready-for-dev or validating}. {Brief summary of key findings and fixes}
2. Replace body of {memory_path}/validator-sidecar/conversation-context.md (preserve header above first ---): Last session: Story validation of {story_key} ({story_title}). Date: {YYYY-MM-DD}. Result: {PASS/FAIL}. Attempts: {N}. Findings: {count}. Fixed: {fixed_count}.
If _memory/validator-sidecar/ directory or files don't exist, create them with standard headers: decision-log.md: "# Val Validator — Decision Log

> Chronological record of validation decisions.

---" conversation-context.md: "# Val Validator — Conversation Context

> Rolling summary of the most recent validation session.

---

No sessions recorded yet." If writing fails, log warning and continue — memory save is non-blocking.

alternative.context: When all stories are created and validated (ready-for-dev)
